guardrail_evaluations.explanation: This deal has no list price or proposed price — it is a rev-share partnership with no subscription fee, so discount_pct is 0% and the discount guardrail does not meaningfully apply. Guardrail passes on the numeric metric but the deal structure is wholly outside the scope of standard PLG pricing rules and requires escalation on those grounds independently.

Same rationale as above — no discount is being granted because there is no subscription fee structure at all. The 0% discount passes this guardrail numerically, but the absence of any floor subscription revenue is itself a yellow flag that the discount framework was not designed to capture.

Because proposed_price is $0, the standard COGS-based gross margin calculation cannot be applied — margin_pct is treated as 0%, which is below the 25% absolute floor. This deal's economics are entirely contingent on Reverberate's downstream rev-share remittances, which are probabilistic ($400K–$1.2M estimated range) and not contractually guaranteed at any floor. The guardrail formally fires as a block_absolute; deal cannot proceed under current structure without CFO/deal desk sign-off on a rev-share exception.

Same structural issue — with no subscription fee, the conventional margin metric reads 0%, well below the 40% warn threshold. The 25% rev-share participation is the margin mechanism here, but it is variable and carries collection risk that a fixed-subscription deal does not.

alternative_structures.label: Hybrid: Minimum Annual Guarantee + Rev Share
alternative_structures.description: Introduce a non-refundable minimum annual guarantee (MAG) of $150K/yr billed upfront, credited against the 25% rev-share pool. If actual rev-share exceeds $150K in a given year, Reverberate remits the overage quarterly as today's proposal envisions. If rev-share falls short, Clay keeps the guarantee. Term: 24 months with a 12-month renewal notice.
alternative_structures.rationale: The MAG converts Clay's upside-only exposure into a floor-revenue structure, guaranteeing $300K TCV over 24 months regardless of Reverberate Engine adoption. At $150K/yr and assuming Clay's COGS on enabling a white-label partner is low (predominantly platform access), this likely lands near or above the 40% margin threshold. Reverberate benefits because the MAG counts toward — not on top of — their rev-share obligation, so it functions as a pre-payment rather than an added cost. This resolves the block_absolute guardrail violation.

alternative_structures.label: Tiered Rev Share with Usage Commit Floor
alternative_structures.description: Restructure the flat 25% rev-share into a tiered model: Clay receives 30% on the first $500K of Reverberate Engine subscription revenue annually, 22% on $500K–$1M, and 18% above $1M. Reverberate commits to a minimum of $200K in Clay-attributed subscription revenue per year (i.e., a usage floor), or pays a shortfall fee equal to 30% of the gap. No upfront subscription fee.
alternative_structures.rationale: The shortfall fee on the $200K commit floor guarantees Clay at minimum $60K/yr (30% × $200K), resolving the zero-floor risk while keeping total cost of partnership lower for Reverberate if they scale quickly (the rate steps down). The higher take rate at low volumes incentivizes Reverberate to grow their book faster to hit the rate reduction threshold. This structure also gives Clay's finance team a contractually enforceable minimum to book against, which is essential for a partnership with no direct subscription fee.

alternative_structures.label: White-Label License Fee + Reduced Rev Share + Co-Marketing Equity
alternative_structures.description: Charge a flat white-label platform license of $75K/yr (billed annually) in exchange for reducing the rev-share rate from 25% to 15%. Add a formal co-marketing obligation: Reverberate must produce 2 Clay-attributed case studies per year featuring named top-50 clients, and include Clay in at least 2 co-branded webinars/events. Early access window reduced from 30 days to 15 days, mitigating IP leakage risk ahead of public launches.
alternative_structures.rationale: The license fee provides Clay with $150K TCV floor over 24 months that is entirely independent of Reverberate's commercial success with their end clients. Cutting the rev-share from 25% to 15% reduces Clay's upside on strong performance years, but the license fee more than compensates at lower revenue scenarios while still being attractive at high volumes. The co-marketing obligation converts the distribution value of Reverberate's 8 top-50 client relationships into a contractual deliverable with named attribution, which has measurable pipeline value for Clay's enterprise motion. Reducing early access to 15 days lowers the risk of feature exposure ahead of GA.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal is structurally outside the scope of Clay's standard pricing guardrails: list_price and proposed_price are both $0, making discount_pct and margin_pct both 0% by conventional calculation. The gross margin absolute floor guardrail (≥25%) formally fires as a block_absolute and cannot be cleared without a CFO/deal desk exception or structural renegotiation. All margin estimates in the alternative structures are working assumptions derived from the standard 40% gross margin at list price baseline called out in the system prompt — they should not be taken as auditable figures until Clay's finance team models actual COGS for white-label partner enablement. No similar past deals were directionally relevant (precedent set consists of standard subscription/expansion deals with no partnership or rev-share comps). The deal has genuine strategic merit — distribution leverage across 8 top-50 customers, conversion of a rebuild risk, and co-marketing upside — but requires a formal partnership framework exception before approval.

performance_obligations.name: White-label license to Clay tables and waterfalls (Reverberate Engine)
performance_obligations.description: Grant of rights for Reverberate to embed and resell Clay's tables and waterfall enrichment functionality under the 'Reverberate Engine' brand to its clients over the 24-month term.
performance_obligations.rationale: The white-label right is distinct (capable of being distinct and separately identifiable) and the consideration is fully variable based on end-client subscriptions; ASC 606-10-32-40 sales-/usage-based royalty exception applies, so revenue is recognized as the underlying client subscriptions occur.

performance_obligations.name: Co-marketing rights and case studies
performance_obligations.description: Mutual co-marketing including case studies that name Reverberate's clients as Clay references.
performance_obligations.rationale: Co-marketing is a marketing/promotional activity that is not a distinct good or service transferred to the customer in the ASC 606 sense; treated as a non-revenue marketing arrangement unless a separable deliverable emerges.

performance_obligations.name: Early access to Clay features (30-day advance)
performance_obligations.description: Reverberate receives access to new Clay product features 30 days before general availability.
performance_obligations.rationale: Early access is a stand-ready obligation distinct from the white-label license; recognized ratably across the 24-month term as the obligation is satisfied over time.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: All Clay consideration is a 25% royalty on Reverberate Engine subscriptions; under the sales-/usage-based royalty exception for licenses of IP, recognize as Reverberate's underlying client sales occur, not estimated upfront.
Naming Reverberate's clients in case studies could create a consideration-payable-or-receivable evaluation; if Clay provides marketing value to Reverberate's clients, may need separate accounting.
Stand-ready obligation has no explicit fee; with zero baseline price and royalty-only consideration, allocation is nominal but should be documented.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: Because there is no fixed fee and the deal is entirely royalty-based with novel white-label scope, any change to rev-share %, scope of bundled features, or early-access window would likely constitute a contract modification under ASC 606-10-25-10. The 8 overlapping top-50 customers also create risk that existing direct contracts get renegotiated/cannibalized, triggering modification analysis on those separate agreements.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Midpoint estimate of $400K–$1.2M annual rev share; recognized as Reverberate's client subscriptions are billed (royalty exception). Actuals will vary.
Midpoint estimate; royalty revenue recognized in the period the underlying sales occur.

red_flags.label: No fixed transaction price — pure royalty deal
red_flags.explanation: TCV/ACV are zero with all consideration variable. Requires CFO/Controller policy decision on royalty exception applicability and forecast disclosure approach.

red_flags.label: Channel conflict / cannibalization of existing direct ARR
red_flags.explanation: 8 of Clay's top-50 customers are Reverberate clients; migrating them to Reverberate Engine could constitute modifications or terminations of existing Clay contracts and accelerate/reverse previously recognized revenue.

red_flags.label: White-label IP license scope undefined
red_flags.explanation: Distinguishing functional vs. symbolic IP under ASC 606-10-55-54 affects point-in-time vs. over-time recognition for any non-royalty components; license scope must be precisely drafted.

red_flags.label: Co-marketing with client attribution
red_flags.explanation: Using Reverberate's clients in Clay case studies may create consideration-payable-to-a-customer issues and legal/privacy exposure; needs legal + accounting review.

red_flags.label: Early-access obligation
red_flags.explanation: 30-day advance access is a stand-ready performance obligation that should be tracked even though its allocated price is nominal.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a pure royalty/rev-share white-label partnership with no fixed fee, multiple distinct performance obligations (white-label IP license, early access stand-ready), and significant contract-modification and channel-conflict risk against existing direct ARR with overlapping customers. Revenue should generally follow the ASC 606-10-32-40 sales-/usage-based royalty exception, recognized as Reverberate's end-client subscriptions occur, but allocation between the license and the stand-ready early-access obligation must be documented. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes to white-label Clay tables and waterfalls under their own branded product 'Reverberate Engine,' selling it as their own product to clients with no Clay attribution required.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A full white-label grant creates an IP sublicensing risk: Reverberate's end-clients may come to believe Reverberate Engine is proprietary to Reverberate, making Clay invisible and displaceable. If Reverberate is acquired or pivots, the white-label right travels with the acquirer unless explicitly restricted. Additionally, Clay loses the ability to upsell or convert Reverberate's end-clients to direct Clay relationships during the term or after.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay grants Reverberate a limited, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to co-brand Clay's tables and waterfall functionality under the 'Reverberate Engine' name solely for delivery of services to Reverberate's direct clients during the Term. (a) All end-client agreements must disclose Clay as the underlying technology provider. (b) The white-label right is personal to Reverberate and does not transfer in connection with any change of control, merger, or acquisition without Clay's prior written consent. (c) Clay retains the right to directly solicit and contract with any Reverberate end-client upon expiration or termination of this Agreement. (d) Reverberate may not grant any sublicense of Clay functionality to end-clients beyond the scope of delivery of the Reverberate Engine service.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate rejects mandatory end-client Clay attribution, accept a 'Powered by Clay' badge requirement in the Reverberate Engine UI and documentation, with the change-of-control consent right and direct-solicit right preserved as non-negotiable.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has not previously executed a full white-label partnership agreement; this clause type is novel for Clay's standard approval matrix and requires legal and executive sign-off.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes a 25% revenue share paid quarterly (net-45 from quarter close) on all Reverberate Engine subscription revenue from their clients, with no baseline subscription fee or minimum guarantee to Clay.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A pure rev-share with no floor exposes Clay to zero-revenue outcomes if Reverberate Engine subscriptions underperform, are discounted away, or are bundled into Reverberate's agency retainers at $0 listed price. Net-45 from quarter close on a quarterly cadence means Clay could wait up to ~135 days for revenue recognition. Without audit rights, Clay cannot verify the subscription revenue base being reported. The estimated $400K–$1.2M range is entirely Reverberate-controlled and unverifiable without contractual protections.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Revenue Share shall be calculated as 25% of Reverberate Engine Subscription Revenue, defined as gross subscription fees actually collected from end-clients attributable to Clay-powered functionality, excluding (a) professional services fees, (b) implementation or onboarding fees, and (c) any fees for Reverberate's proprietary non-Clay modules. Reverberate shall remit payment within 45 days of each calendar quarter-end and shall accompany each remittance with a written revenue report itemizing end-clients, contract values, and collected amounts. Clay reserves the right to audit Reverberate's relevant books and records, with no more than one audit per calendar year and 10 business days' written notice. A minimum annual revenue share guarantee of $150,000 shall apply commencing at month 13 of the Term; failure to meet this minimum entitles Clay to convert the partnership to a standard reseller agreement or terminate with 60 days' notice.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate rejects the $150K annual minimum, accept a mutual good-faith annual business review with renegotiation rights if trailing-twelve-month rev share falls below $100K, and retain audit rights as non-negotiable.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has executed referral fee arrangements but not a structured rev-share with audit rights; the audit right and minimum guarantee language should be treated as novel and reviewed by Finance.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests co-marketing rights including the ability to publish case studies featuring their clients, with attribution to Reverberate as the operator — implying Clay content may reference Reverberate's end-clients.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Co-marketing rights that allow Reverberate to publish case studies about their end-clients using Clay create a layered consent problem: Clay would be referenced in marketing about clients who never contracted with Clay and may not have consented to vendor-level attribution. If a Reverberate end-client is a Clay direct prospect or existing customer, a Reverberate-attributed case study could complicate Clay's own GTM motions and create confusion about who owns the relationship.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Co-marketing activities, including case studies, joint press releases, and social content, shall require (a) prior written approval from Clay's marketing team on a case-by-case basis, not to be unreasonably withheld, (b) written consent from the relevant Reverberate end-client prior to publication, and (c) attribution language that identifies both Clay and Reverberate as co-contributors (e.g., 'Built on Clay, delivered by Reverberate Engine'). Clay retains the right to separately publish its own case studies about any end-client that is also a direct Clay customer, independent of this Agreement.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate pushes back on case-by-case Clay approval, accept a 10-business-day review window with deemed approval on silence, but retain the end-client written consent requirement and dual-attribution language as non-negotiable.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay's standard co-marketing addendum requires case-by-case approval and dual attribution; this clause is close to standard but the layered end-client consent requirement is an extension not previously codified.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests 30-day advance access to all new Clay features prior to public launch.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A blanket 30-day early-access right for all features creates execution risk on two dimensions: (1) Clay may ship features in limited beta that are not partner-ready, and pre-launch exposure to Reverberate's 50+ clients could surface bugs or incomplete UX at scale; (2) Reverberate's founder's high-profile content platform (14k-subscriber playbook) creates a material risk that early-access features are disclosed publicly before launch, compromising Clay's product announcement sequencing. The founder's 'vendor-agnostic' public positioning increases the likelihood of inadvertent or strategic disclosure.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Reverberate with early access to new product features designated by Clay as 'partner preview' features no fewer than 14 days prior to general availability, subject to (a) execution of Clay's standard beta NDA and early access terms, (b) Clay's sole discretion to exclude features that are in limited alpha, subject to third-party licensing restrictions, or carry security or stability risk, and (c) Reverberate's obligation to restrict access to no more than 5 named internal Reverberate staff during the preview period. Reverberate may not disclose, publish, or demonstrate early-access features to end-clients or publicly prior to Clay's general availability announcement.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate insists on 30 days, accept 30-day early access for partner-designated features only (not all features), with the NDA, 5-named-user cap, and non-disclosure obligation preserved.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered beta access to select enterprise customers under NDA; a formal 30-day partner early-access right as a contractual entitlement has not previously been granted.

standard_clauses_affirmed: Payment model: custom rev-share remittance, no standard subscription — explicitly noted and routed to RevOps
No discount or list-price deviation (zero-ACV partnership structure)
redline.one_line_summary: We cannot ship this as-is — the uncapped white-label grant and no-floor rev-share must be redlined before signature: white-label needs change-of-control protection and mandatory Clay attribution, and rev-share needs audit rights, a defined revenue base, and a Year 2 minimum guarantee.
redline.reasoning_summary: This deal is structurally novel for Clay: a full white-label partnership with a rev-share-only commercial model, no precedent in the standard approval matrix. The two blocking issues are (1) the white-label grant is unlimited in scope and survives change-of-control without restriction, which could transfer Clay's IP to an acquirer without consent, and (2) the rev-share has no floor, no audit rights, and no defined revenue base, making Clay's actual yield entirely dependent on Reverberate's unverifiable self-reporting. The co-marketing and early-access terms are workable with standard redlines but the NDA and attribution controls are non-negotiable given the founder's high-profile public platform. Legal and executive sign-off required before any counter is delivered to Reverberate.

required_approvers.rationale: Deal type is partnership; CEO approval is mandatory for all partnership structures per matrix default rule.

White-label IP grant, rev-share with audit rights, co-marketing with client attribution, and early-access terms all require Legal review; Redline Agent flagged overall priority as 'block' due to uncapped white-label scope and no-floor rev-share exposure.

Zero fixed price and pure rev-share structure ($400K–$1.2M estimated annual range, unverifiable) triggers CFO sign-off on rev-share exception; Pricing Agent flagged gross margin absolute floor as block_absolute; ASC 606 Agent flagged no fixed transaction price and channel conflict as block-without-approval red flags.

Net-45 quarterly remittance cadence and rev-share audit/verification mechanics require Finance review per Pricing Agent guardrail block_absolute and Redline Agent risk on unverifiable subscription revenue base.

Novel white-label partnership with distribution leverage across 8 top-50 customers and channel-conflict risk with existing direct ARR; requires structured partnership framework exception beyond standard approval matrix.

blockers_to_address_first: Redline white-label grant: must add change-of-control consent requirement, non-transferability clause, and mandatory 'Powered by Clay' attribution or 'Clay-disclosed-to-end-clients' language before Legal will approve.

Redline rev-share: must add minimum annual guarantee ($150K/yr proposed in Pricing alternative structures) or mutual good-faith renegotiation trigger, plus audit rights and defined revenue base (gross subscription fees excluding services/onboarding), before Finance/CFO will approve.

Pricing Agent block_absolute (gross margin floor): deal cannot proceed under current zero-ACV structure without CFO exception or renegotiation to a Hybrid MAG + Rev Share structure (e.g., $150K/yr MAG credited against 25% rev-share) that provides floor revenue and 40%+ margin visibility.

ASC 606 red flag (channel conflict): must obtain CFO/controller policy decision on revenue recognition treatment for royalty exception applicability and documentation of existing-customer cannibalization risk (8 overlapping top-50 customers); requires contract-modification-risk mitigation clause in final agreement.

ASC 606 red flag (no fixed transaction price): Accounting policy memo required from CFO/Controller on ASC 606-10-32-40 royalty exception applicability and forecast/disclosure approach for zero-ACV partnership before deal signature.

Co-marketing IP exposure: must obtain written consent protocol from Reverberate on case-by-case Clay marketing approval and end-client opt-in before case studies are published; cannot reference unnamed Reverberate clients in Clay marketing without their consent.

Early-access NDA and feature cap: must finalize NDA with 5-named-user cap and non-disclosure obligation, plus Clay's right to exclude alpha/restricted features before partner early-access entitlements are triggered.
approval.one_line_summary: This routes to AE Manager → Legal + CFO + Finance (parallel) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO. Expect ~21 business days. Must redline white-label grant for change-of-control + mandatory Clay attribution, add rev-share minimum annual guarantee + audit rights, obtain CFO exception on zero-ACV margin floor, and resolve channel-conflict risk with overlapping top-50 customers before submission or Legal and CFO will reject.
approval.reasoning_summary: Partnership deals are matrix defaults routed to CEO; this one triggers CEO, Legal, CFO, Finance, and Strategic Partnership Review due to novel white-label IP scope, zero-price rev-share structure, and channel-conflict exposure. Redline Agent flagged overall priority as 'block' due to uncapped white-label grant (change-of-control/sublicense/upsell risk) and no-floor rev-share (zero-revenue exposure, unverifiable self-reporting). Pricing Agent flagged gross margin absolute floor as block_absolute; ASC 606 flagged contract-modification and cannibalization risk as block-without-approval. Standard approval matrix does not apply to partnership deals; deal requires formal exception framework, redline negotiation, and CFO policy decision on rev-share revenue recognition before any step of the approval chain can proceed.

text.text: Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership — Reverberate Growth

fields.text:
*ACV / TCV*
$0 fixed — est. $400K–$1.2M/yr via 25% rev share

*Approval Chain*
AE Manager → Legal + CFO + Finance (parallel) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO (~21 biz days)

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know*

• *Cannot proceed as drafted — two blocking redlines must be resolved first.* The white-label grant is uncapped and survives change-of-control with no Clay attribution requirement; and the rev-share has zero floor, no audit rights, and no defined revenue base. Legal and CFO will reject without these fixes. Pricing Agent's recommended path is a Hybrid MAG ($150K/yr, credited against rev share) to clear the gross margin block_absolute.

• *Channel conflict is a material risk.* 8 of Clay's top-50 direct customers are Reverberate clients. Migrating them to Reverberate Engine could trigger contract modifications or terminations on existing Clay ARR, and the ASC 606 agent flagged this as block-without-approval pending a CFO/Controller policy decision on rev recognition and a cannibalization mitigation clause.

• *This deal has genuine strategic upside but is structurally novel — no prior precedent in Clay's approval matrix.* Reverberate is ranked #18 on the Top 50 GTM Agencies list (up from #34), has 50+ active mid-market clients, and a high-profile founder. A formal partnership framework exception is required before any approval step can proceed; standard matrix does not apply.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Devon Wright (Mgr: Sarah Goldstein)   |   review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership — Reverberate Growth
Deal Type: Partnership | ACV: $0 fixed, est. $400K–$1.2M/yr (25% rev share) | Term: 24 months
Approval Chain: AE Manager → Legal + CFO + Finance (parallel) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO (~21 biz days)
Key issues: Uncapped white-label grant and zero-floor rev share must be redlined before submission; 8 overlapping top-50 customers create channel conflict and ASC 606 risk requiring CFO policy decision; no prior partnership precedent — formal framework exception required.
AE Owner: Devon Wright | Mgr: Sarah Goldstein | Review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Reverberate] Deal desk review complete — 3 blockers before you can submit for approval
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Devon, deal desk has reviewed the Reverberate Engine white-label partnership and it is **blocked from submission** in its current form — three hard issues must be resolved before any approver will touch it.

1. **Redline the white-label grant.** The current language is uncapped and survives change-of-control. Get Legal's counter in front of Reverberate: non-transferable, non-sublicensable, mandatory 'Powered by Clay' attribution (or end-client disclosure), and a change-of-control consent right. This is a veto risk for Legal.
2. **Restructure the rev-share to include a floor.** A pure 25% rev-share with no minimum and no audit rights will be rejected by CFO and Finance. Bring Reverberate a Hybrid MAG option: $150K/yr non-refundable minimum, credited against the rev-share pool, plus quarterly revenue reports and annual audit rights. This resolves the pricing block_absolute guardrail.
3. **Flag the 8 overlapping top-50 customers to your AE Manager (Sarah) now.** Channel-conflict and ASC 606 cannibalization risk on existing direct ARR requires a CFO/Controller policy memo before deal signature — the earlier Finance is looped in, the less this delays the close.

Approval chain once blockers are cleared: **Sarah (Step 1) → Legal + CFO + Finance in parallel (Step 2) → Strategic Partnership Review (Step 3) → CEO (Step 4).** Budget ~21 business days from clean submission. June 1 close is at risk without immediate action on the redlines.

customer_email_draft.subject: Reverberate Engine Partnership — Clay's positions on the four headline asks
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We genuinely appreciate the thoughtfulness behind the Reverberate Engine proposal, and the strategic logic is clear on both sides — your distribution reach across top-50 GTM clients and Clay's infrastructure are a natural fit. That said, because this structure is novel for Clay (no precedent in our standard approval matrix), we need to work through four specific positions before we can route this for executive sign-off.

**White-label grant:** We can support co-branding under the Reverberate Engine name, but we cannot grant a silent, fully transferable white-label right. Our counter requires a 'Powered by Clay' disclosure in the product UI and documentation, a change-of-control consent requirement (the license does not travel to an acquirer without Clay's written approval), and Clay's right to solicit end-clients directly upon expiration or termination.

**Rev-share rate and structure:** We're open to the 25% rate, but we need three additions: (1) a defined revenue base — gross subscription fees actually collected, excluding professional services and onboarding; (2) quarterly remittance accompanied by a written revenue report with end-client and contract-level detail; and (3) Clay audit rights (once per calendar year, 10 business days' notice). We're also proposing a minimum annual guarantee of $150K beginning in month 13, structured as a credit against rev-share — so it functions as a pre-payment, not an added cost, if you're scaling as expected.

**Co-marketing and case studies:** We want these to happen, and we'll make Clay's approval process fast (10 business days, deemed approved on silence). What we need is case-by-case sign-off from Clay marketing, written consent from the named end-client, and dual attribution language along the lines of 'Built on Clay, delivered by Reverberate Engine.'

**Early access:** We can offer 14 days on Clay-designated 'partner preview' features, subject to a standard beta NDA, a 5-named-user cap internally, and a non-disclosure obligation before GA. A blanket 30-day right across all features — including alpha and restricted builds — creates execution and disclosure risk we can't accept given the public profile of your content platform; if 30 days is important to you, we can discuss scoping it to a defined partner-preview tier rather than all features.

Our internal routing (Legal, CFO, Finance, and Strategic Partnership Review in parallel, then CEO) will run approximately 21 business days once we have aligned term sheet language — so the sooner we can align on these four points, the more runway we have before your June 1 target. Can we get 45 minutes this week to walk through the white-label and rev-share positions specifically?

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Reverberate Growth (#18 top GTM agency, 80 employees, $12M ARR) proposes a 24-month white-label partnership: Clay powers their 'Reverberate Engine' product, sold to their 50+ mid-market clients, in exchange for a 25% revenue share (est. $400K–$1.2M/yr) with no baseline subscription fee, plus co-marketing rights and 30-day early feature access. AE Devon Wright lacks authority; routed from RevOps. Close target: June 1, 2026.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
- **List price / ACV / TCV:** $0 — pure rev-share, no subscription fee
- **Effective discount:** N/A (no priced structure)
- **Estimated Clay yield:** $400K–$1.2M/yr (midpoint ~$800K); entirely variable, unverifiable without audit rights
- **Margin:** Conventional margin = 0% → **fires `block_absolute` gross margin guardrail (floor: 25%)**
- **Recommended alternative structures (pick one before re-submission):**
  1. **Hybrid MAG + Rev Share** — $150K/yr non-refundable minimum annual guarantee credited against 25% rev share; resolves margin block; ~40% margin est.
  2. **Tiered Rev Share + Usage Commit Floor** — 30%/22%/18% tiers; $200K/yr commit floor (~$60K/yr floor guarantee); ~35% margin est.
  3. **White-Label License Fee + Reduced Rev Share** — $75K/yr flat license; rev share drops 25% → 15%; ~42% margin est.; lowest Reverberate upside risk

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**🔴 Block — Must Resolve Before Submission**

| # | Source | Finding |
|---|--------|---------|
| 1 | Redline | **Uncapped white-label grant** — no change-of-control restriction; Clay IP transfers to any Reverberate acquirer without consent. Counter required: non-transferable license + CoC consent right + mandatory Clay attribution. |
| 2 | Redline | **No-floor rev share** — zero-revenue outcome possible; no audit rights; revenue base undefined (services vs. subscriptions). Counter required: $150K MAG or $100K renegotiation trigger + audit rights + defined revenue base. |
| 3 | Pricing | **Gross margin `block_absolute`** — $0 proposed price reads as 0% margin vs. 25% floor. CFO exception or structural renegotiation required before proceeding. |
| 4 | ASC 606 | **No fixed transaction price** — pure royalty; CFO/Controller policy memo required on ASC 606-10-32-40 royalty exception applicability and forecast disclosure approach. |
| 5 | ASC 606 | **Channel conflict / cannibalization** — 8 of Clay's top-50 direct customers are Reverberate clients; migration to Reverberate Engine could trigger contract modification/termination analysis and reverse previously recognized revenue. |

**🟡 Warn — Address in Negotiation**

| # | Source | Finding |
|---|--------|---------|
| 6 | Redline | **Co-marketing client attribution** — naming Reverberate's end-clients in Clay case studies requires layered consent (end-client opt-in + Clay marketing approval per case study). Consideration-payable-to-customer ASC 606 evaluation needed. |
| 7 | Redline | **Early access (30-day blanket)** — founder's 14K-subscriber platform creates pre-launch disclosure risk. Counter: 14–30 days for 'partner preview' features only; 5-named-user cap; NDA + non-disclosure obligation. |
| 8 | ASC 606 | **White-label IP license scope undefined** — functional vs. symbolic IP distinction affects recognition pattern; precise drafting required. |

**ℹ️ Info**
- Early-access is a stand-ready PO; allocation is nominal but must be documented per ASC 606.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|-------|
| 1 | AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) | No | Confirm deal handoff from RevOps |
| 2 | Legal + CFO + Finance | Yes (Legal, CFO) | Parallel; Legal on white-label/IP; CFO on margin block + rev recognition; Finance on audit/remittance mechanics |
| 3 | Strategic Partnership Review | Yes | Novel framework exception; no prior white-label precedent |
| 4 | CEO | Yes | Required for all partnership deal types |

**Estimated cycle time: ~21 business days** (assuming redlines are delivered to Reverberate before Step 1 — delays in counter negotiation will extend timeline beyond June 1 close target).

⚠️ The June 1 close date is at risk given the 21-day approval cycle and open redline negotiation required before submission.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**⛔ Hold — Ship with Conditions after mandatory redlines**

Do not submit for approval in current form. Return to Reverberate with redlined terms on (1) white-label grant scope + change-of-control protection, (2) rev-share minimum annual guarantee + audit rights, and (3) one of the three alternative pricing structures — then re-route once Legal confirms redlines are acceptable and CFO issues the margin-floor exception memo. Strategic upside (distribution leverage across 8 top-50 customers, conversion of rebuild risk) is real, but current zero-floor, uncapped structure creates IP and revenue exposure that Legal, CFO, and Strategic Partnership Review will reject outright.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to collaborative tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~21 business days through 6-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing falls outside standard guardrails with $0 list and $0 proposed price triggering a block_absolute on the 25% gross-margin floor, so any approval requires a formal CFO/deal desk exception rather than a discount call. ASC 606 treats this as a sales-based royalty under 32-40 with multiple distinct obligations and live contract-modification plus channel-conflict risk against overlapping direct ARR, pending CFO and auditor sign-off. Redline is a hard block on two issues: an unlimited white-label grant that survives change-of-control and a rev-share with no floor, no audit rights, and no defined revenue base. Approval routes through a non-standard six-step chain (AE manager → legal → CFO → finance → strategic partnership review → CEO) at roughly 21 business days, and the call is hold and re-scope — fix the IP grant and rev-share floor before any counter goes back to Reverberate.

Gong RevOps team led the eval. Standard enterprise structure with two non-standard clauses (SLA + security review). Closed in Q1 with quick legal turn.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth crosses 50 active mid-market clients, formalizes 'Reverberate Engine' practice
signals.summary: GTM agency Reverberate Growth announced its 50th active client and the formal launch of 'Reverberate Engine,' a productized service line that bundles outbound and enrichment workflows for mid-market RevOps teams.

signals.headline: Reverberate founder publishes 2026 GTM Operator Playbook, 14k subscribers in two weeks
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth's founder released the '2026 GTM Operator Playbook,' a long-form essay on agency-led RevOps that gained 14,000 subscribers in its first two weeks. The post argues for vendor-agnostic agency partnerships over single-vendor lock-in.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth named #18 on annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth ranked #18 on the annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list (up from #34 the prior year), with judges citing strong client outcomes in enrichment-heavy outbound and waterfall enrichment design.

signals.headline: GTM agencies announce vendor partnerships at 2026 RevOps Summit
signals.summary: At the RevOps Summit, several top-50 GTM agencies — including Reverberate — were named in vendor co-launch sessions. Industry observers describe a shift toward agency-vendor revenue-share partnerships rather than referral fees.

signals.headline: Reverberate hires former PLG-vendor head of partnerships
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth hired a former head of partnerships from a major PLG vendor to lead its new 'Strategic Vendor Partnerships' practice — explicitly framed as a path to revenue-share deals with infrastructure providers.
